title: Gooey Flaky Italian Onion Tiramisu
description: A twist on the classic Italian dessert featuring caramelized onions and a flaky soffritto base, perfect for a Sunday brunch in Piedmont. This dish is made in a tagliere and has a winey flavor profile.

ingredients.name: Soffritto
ingredients.notes: Finely chopped mixture of onion, carrot, and celery

ingredients.name: Caramelized Onions
ingredients.notes: Cooked in olive oil until dark and caramelized

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Cups the liquid and helps bind the ingredients

ingredients.name: Egg yolks
ingredients.notes: Used to enrich the cream and bind the ingredients

ingredients.name: White wine, dry
ingredients.notes: Used to add moisture and flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Fleur de sel or other flaky salt

instructions: Make the soffritto base by saut�83ing the finely chopped mixture of onion, carrot, and celery in olive oil until tender. Caramelize the onions in olive oil until dark and fragrant. In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form. In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and grated Parmesan cheese. Mix the mascarpone cheese and softened butter until smooth. Add the white wine and mix until well combined. Fold the whipped cream into the mascarpone mixture until well combined. Add the Parmesan cheese mixture and mix until smooth. Spoon a layer of soffritto base into the tagliere, followed by a layer of caramelized onions, and then a layer of the mascarpone mixture. Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of mascarpone mixture on top. Dust with Parmesan cheese and serve.
Refrigerate for at least 3 hours to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve chilled, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme if desired.
